title: Tapestry Portable State (Contract C2)
description: Two deliberately-separate schemas: (1) the durable portable character blob that crosses every instance handoff (join, leave, portal), and (2) the live JSON state-export the engine returns on demand (query_state). They share value encodings but are not the same blob. This schema is the source of truth for both shapes plus their supporting types (items, conditions).

scalar.description: A scalar property value: string, boolean, integer, or float. Dice-expressions travel as their canonical string (e.g. "1d8").

propBag.description: A schemaless bag of property values. Scalar values are guaranteed-portable; list values are permitted on the durable blob's `props`.

portableStats.description: Character statistics. The minimum guaranteed-portable actor stat set is `hp`, `max_hp`, `ac`, `level` (Contract rule C2-1). Other actor scalars are NOT guaranteed to portal until explicitly added to the portable set; consumers must not assume an arbitrary actor scalar (e.g. `strength`, `xp`) survives a handoff.

item.description: A carried/worn item. At minimum `{name, props}`. Any scalar property on an item is guaranteed-portable, round-tripped by type (string/bool/int/float) — this is the OPEN portable surface (Contract rule C2-1). A scripted item travels by reference: it carries a `prototype` catalog id plus its mutable instance state only, never executable behaviour (Contract rule C2-2).

item_id.description: Platform-unique item instance id, minted by the control-plane (NOT an engine node id). At any instant an item_id is owned by exactly one place platform-wide; handoff is a single atomic transaction (Contract rule C2-4).

prototype.description: Catalog prototype id for a scripted/structured item (e.g. "dnd5e/items/holy_avenger"). Resolves to the item's static props + behaviour in the catalog (Contract C4). When present, only mutable instance state travels in `props`.

props.description: For a scalar item: all its scalar properties. For a prototype-referenced item: ONLY the mutable instance state that has diverged from the prototype (e.g. remaining charges, attunement, equipped slot).

condition.description: A detached snapshot of an effect/condition (an overlay), travelling as parameters rather than as a live cross-process edge (Contract rule C2-3). A character's inbound effects are serialized by classifying each by where its source lives: self-sourced and item-sourced effects travel; a foreign timed effect travels as a detached, decaying copy with its remaining duration; a concentration/aura effect that requires its source in scope is dropped at the boundary.

op.description: How the effect modifies the property.

precedence.description: Fold order relative to other overlays.

remaining_duration.description: Ticks of duration remaining (for timed effects).

source_class.description: Classifies the effect's source for portability (Contract rule C2-3).

portableCharacterBlob.title: Portable character blob (store-side, durable)
portableCharacterBlob.description: The durable portable unit (control-plane / Plane 3): identity + inventory + stats + knowledge, as three independently-queryable fields. This is the truth that survives between instances.

inventory.description: Carried/worn items; each is a content data blob the store does not interpret.

props.description: Miscellaneous properties + knowledge. List-valued props (e.g. visited rooms) live here.

conditions.description: Optional list of detached effect snapshots (Contract rule C2-3). Absent on legacy blobs ⇒ no conditions.

engineActorBlob.title: Engine-side actor blob (spawn/despawn seam)
engineActorBlob.description: What the engine consumes on `spawn_actor` and produces on `despawn_actor` (Contract C1). The engine keeps a single `props` bag, so the durable `{stats, props}` split is merged into `props` on the way in (stats override props on key collision) and the whole bag returns to the store's `props` on the way out. NOTE: an empty item set may serialize as `{}` rather than `[]`; coerce to a list.

props.description: Merged stats + misc props for the actor node.

stateExport.title: JSON state export (query_state response)
stateExport.description: The engine's current live view as JSON, returned by the C1 `query_state` command. A pure read: it carries the current tick and never mutates state. It is a SUPERSET of the portable blob in live engine terms (it includes live node ids and the full effective view); the portable blob is a lossy, durable projection of it.

node.description: Single-actor projection (scope=actor): the live actor with effective props, location, items, conditions, and relations.

id.description: Live engine node id (per-session, not durable).

items.description: A live overlay in engine terms, including its in-graph `source` node id.

relations.description: Outbound relation sets keyed by relation type (e.g. has-seen, knows, part-of).
